query_normalized: evaluating the impact of code generation on long-term code maintainability computer science
rationale: This paper focuses on scientific data and code preservation for reproducibility (likely in high-energy physics given "Snowmass 2021"), which is a distinct construct from software engineering maintainability regarding AI code generation. It shares only homonym keywords ("code", "long-term") without addressing the user's specific mechanism (code generation impact) or domain (software evolution/maintainability).
